'''Richard Henare''' (born 12 October 1968)<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> is a New Zealand former [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played at club level for [[Carlisle RLFC|Carlisle]], in the [[Super League]] for the [[Warrington Wolves]], and the [[Swinton Lions]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Playing career==
Henare began his career playing for [[Carlisle RLFC|Carlisle]] before joining the [[Warrington Wolves]] in 1996.<ref name="Leeds chase Stuart">{{cite web|url=http://www.thefreelibrary.com/Leeds+chase+Stuart.-a061336462|title=Leeds chase Stuart|publisher=thefreelibrary.com|date=28 February 1996|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref> In the 1996 and 1997 seasons he played twenty-four [[Super League]] matches for the Warrington Wolves.<ref name="Ropati inspires Auckland triumph">{{cite web|url=http://www.independent.co.uk/sport/ropati-inspires-auckland-triumph-1257753.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121111114049/http://www.independent.co.uk/sport/ropati-inspires-auckland-triumph-1257753.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=11 November 2012|title=Ropati inspires Auckland triumph|publisher=independent.co.uk|date=24 June 1997|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref>

Richard Henare made his début for [[Warrington Wolves]] on Friday 5 April 1996, and he played his last match for Warrington Wolves on Friday 11 July 1997, he was Warrington Wolves' leading try-scorer in 1996's [[Super League I]] with 17-[[Try (rugby)|tries]].{{citation needed|date=February 2024}}

He later played for [[Swinton Lions]] before being released in 2000 after receiving a twelve-month ban for testing positive for cannabis.<ref name="Swinton set to release Henare for drug ban">{{cite web|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/swinton-set-to-release-henare-for-drug-ban-700432.html|title=Swinton set to release Henare for drug ban|publisher=independent.co.uk|date=5 September 2000|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref>
